seoTitle: How to Add Tags in Airtable, Create Easily
metaDescription: Create color-coded tags in Airtable using a Multiple Select field, then filter and group your records by the tags you've assigned.
intro: Tags in Airtable aren't a separate feature — they're built using a Multiple Select or Single Select field, which lets you assign custom color-coded labels and then filter or group records by them. This guide covers setting up the field, customizing tag colors, and using them to organize your data.

steps.heading: Open your target table
steps.body: Navigate to the table where you want to add tagging to your records.

steps.heading: Add a Multiple Select field
steps.body: Click Add Field and choose the Multiple Select data type, which allows records to hold more than one tag at once.

steps.heading: Create your tag labels
steps.body: Type in custom text labels within the field configuration to serve as your available tags.

steps.heading: Assign colors to each tag
steps.body: Customize the color of each individual tag to build a visually consistent categorization system across your grid.

steps.heading: Apply tags to records
steps.body: Use the dropdown selector on each row to apply one or more tags to that record.

steps.heading: Filter and group by tags
steps.body: Use grid filters and grouping tools to sort your database based on which tags are assigned.

faq.question: What's the difference between Single Select and Multiple Select for tags?
faq.answer: Single Select allows only one tag per record, while Multiple Select allows a record to carry several tags at once.

faq.question: Can I change a tag's color after creating it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit any tag's assigned color at any time through the field's configuration menu.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many tags I can create?
faq.answer: Airtable allows a large number of options within a Select field, though very long lists can become harder to manage visually.

faq.question: Can I filter records by multiple tags at once?
faq.answer: Yes, grid filters support matching against multiple selected tag values within a Multiple Select field.
